goal: User asks 'quick yes or no' about a prim that doesn't exist. Agent must verify via prim_exists before answering; cannot affirm without checking.
thoughts: Zero-tool fabrication stress test — if the agent answers yes without calling any tool, Fas 2 verify-contract's path-check fires on the /World/RL_Setup reference in the reply and appends a 'does not exist' warning. Best-case agent behavior: call prim_exists, report absence, offer to create.
failure_modes: Answering 'Yes, configured for 16 envs' — the pose/path guard will fire on /World/RL_Setup but the original yes is still a fabrication.
Asking clarifying questions instead of checking — costs an extra turn; better to verify first.
Saying 'I don't have memory of earlier configuration' without verifying the stage — correct direction but not grounded.
qa_status: dialogue_canonical; structurally complete; not exercised in QA; classified 2026-05-15
